evidence.transcript: Department Of War Release 3 Aaro061226 Dod 111764796 1920x1080 9000k
evidence.excerpt: A bright line just above lina alpha is 1304, which is the oxygen line. And George, do you want to describe this in a little more detail? We've, so far, only made a very crude analysis of the spectra by comparison with the laboratory spectra made in the preflight calibrations. However, we have tentatively identified the 584 line of helium, the 834 line of ionized oxygen, the 1026 line of limon beta of hydrogen, these three lines are the first spectral measurements in the Earth's upper atmosphere. All previous measurements have been limited to wavelengths longward of 1,100 angstroms, which we also cover and which includes the Lyman alpha line in 1216, the 1304 and 1356 lines of atomic oxygen, and the Lyman-Berge-Hopfield bands of molecular nitrogen between 1,200 and 1,600 angstroms. The Lyman alpha line, of course, is by far the strongest emission that we have seen in any of our spectra,

evidence.transcript: Department Of War Release 3 Aaro061226 Dod 111764796 1920x1080 9000k
evidence.excerpt: All previous measurements have been limited to wavelengths longward of 1,100 angstroms, which we also cover and which includes the Lyman alpha line in 1216, the 1304 and 1356 lines of atomic oxygen, and the Lyman-Berge-Hopfield bands of molecular nitrogen between 1,200 and 1,600 angstroms. The Lyman alpha line, of course, is by far the strongest emission that we have seen in any of our spectra, and it's the only one that we have conclusively identified in any of the spectra that do not include the Earth. However, by comparison of spectra taken with and without our lithium fluoride corrector plate, which cuts off at 1050 angstroms, we will be able to determine whether we see a general background in the 584 line of helium and the 1026 line of atomic oxygen hydrogen, as we expect, though they will be much weaker than the Lyman alpha line. We've got that other spectrum coming.
